channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: AWS to invest $11 billion in Georgia to expand infra for gen AI
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) is planning to invest $11 billion in the state of Georgia to expand its infrastructure in the US to support the growing demand for generative AI, AI, and other cloud technologies.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: TikTok alternative Lemon8: Why creators are moving to ByteDance’s other social media platform
item.description: “Okay it’s happening… are we switching to Lemon8 or what’s the plan?” posted one TikTok user back in December. With a potential U.S. ban just days away, content creators scrambling for an alternative are turning their attention to the platform’s sister app, Lemon8.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Meta rolls back hate speech rules as Zuckerberg cites 'recent elections' as a catalyst
item.description: It wasn't just fact-checking that Meta scrapped from its platforms as it prepares for the second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: From AI assistants to holographic displays, automakers showcase in-cabin experiences at CES
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Interior lighting aimed at reducing motion sickness. Shiny holographic dashboards displayed on windshields. And AI-powered voice assistants paired with infotainment systems to keep you company on long drives.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: GlassFish update fixes restart hangs, random 403 responses
item.description: The Eclipse Foundation has released GlassFish 7.0.21, an update of the enterprise Java application server that focuses on fixing a few tenacious bugs.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Federal Cyber Leaders Share Innovation Strategies
item.description: Federal cybersecurity leaders share innovative strategies for staying ahead of threats and protecting sensitive information
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: New League of Legends video might have set up Arcane's sequel series, and now I'm even more desperate to see the hit Netflix show's first follow-up
item.description: A new trailer for Riot Games' hugely popular game series appears to act as a prologue to one of Arcane's sequel projects.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Biden: Biggest regret failing to counter misinformation, including from Trump
item.description: President Biden in an interview published Wednesday reflected on the highs and lows of his administration, stating his biggest regret was failing to challenge misinformation — including claims touted by President-elect Trump about the New Year's Day attacks.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: New research shows a quarter of freshwater animals are threatened with extinction
item.description: New research shows about a quarter of animals living in rivers, lakes and other freshwater sources are threatened with extinction.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: ByteDance's Lemon8 gains traction amid TikTok ban threat as creators push the app
item.description: Hearing a lot about Lemon8 lately? You’re not the only one. Amid a looming U.S. ban on TikTok, content creators have been pushing the platform's sister app.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Website certificates that expire every six weeks? What IT should know
item.description: Industry forces — led by Apple and Google — are pushing for a sharp acceleration of how often website certificates must be updated, but the stated security reason is raising an awful lot of eyebrows.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Flying taxis are on the horizon as aviation soars into a new frontier
item.description: When he was still a boy making the long, tedious trips between his school and his woodsy home in the mountains during the 1980s, JoeBen Bevirt began fantasizing about flying cars that could whisk him to his destination in a matter of minutes.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump says Meta has 'come a long way' after it ends fact-checking program
item.description: President-elect Trump on Tuesday said that social media giant Meta has progressed as a company after it announced that it would eliminate its fact-checking program.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: OpenAI is losing money on its pricey ChatGPT Pro subscription
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, in a post on X, says the AI ​​company is currently losing money on its ChatGPT Pro subscription. “People are using it much more than we expected,” he wrote. The company introduced its ChatGPT Pro subscription in December.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Gunnar Optiks reveals new lenses and gaming-themed frames at CES
item.description: The company that says its products helps with digital eye strain is offering new lenses including a electrochromic ones that changes tint instantly
item.dc:creator: Gieson Cacho, The Mercury News

item.title: Facebook to end fact-checking, boost political content
item.description: The company said automated moderation would focus on serious issues like terrorism and child sexual exploitation instead.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Why Apple’s AI-driven reality distortion matters
item.description: Apple has been forced to admit what every company involved in artificial intelligence (AI) should also be forced to state — AI makes mistakes, just like people do. On the surface, it’s not a terribly big deal: Apple’s AI badly mangled a handful of news headlines.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How adding capacity to a network could reduce IT costs
item.description: Finding an enterprise looking to spend more on network equipment is like finding gold while digging for worms for bait. Cost management used to be an option in enhancing profits, but these days it’s a mantra.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Fubo combining with Disney's Hulu + Live TV; lawsuit against Venu Sports settled
item.description: Disney's Hulu + Live TV and sports streaming service Fubo are combining in a deal that will also see the settlement of a lawsuit against the creation of Venu Sports.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: WordPress.org statement threatens possible shutdown for all of 2025
item.description: Editor’s note: On Jan. 3, 2025, WordPress.org staff announced that they were resuming the suspended services. Automattic CEO Matt Mullenweg on Friday announced a shutdown of almost all services on WordPress.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Someone needs to make AI easy
item.description: 2024 was a year filled with AI possibilities; 2025 needs to turn those possibilities into realities. For that, we need the cloud companies to stop churning out “primitives” such as large language models (LLMs) and instead give developers solutions.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: NASA's Martian helicopter promises unprecedented views of the red planet
item.description: NASA's Ingenuity helicopter, which flew on Mars in April 2021, became the first aircraft to achieve powered, controlled flight on a planet other than Earth.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Turo rentals emerge as common thread in Las Vegas Cybertruck and New Orleans deadly incidents
item.description: Turo faces scrutiny after tragic incidents involving rented vehicles, raising questions about security measures and regulatory frameworks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Apple to pay $95 million to settle lawsuit accusing Siri of eavesdropping
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit accusing the privacy-minded company of deploying its virtual assistant Siri to eavesdrop on people using its iPhone and other trendy devices.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Graviton progress: 50% of new AWS instances run on Amazon custom silicon
item.description: Half of all instances spun up on Amazon Web Services over the past two years used Graviton, Amazon’s custom silicon built on Arm processor technology, rather than x86 processors.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft raises bar with water-saving data center design
item.description: Microsoft’s new data center designs include a cooling system that it claims will consume zero water while at the same time offering liquid cooling of the hardware. The designs leverage a chip-level liquid cooling solution that can provide precise temperature control without water evaporation.
item.dc:creator: Network World
